epics.title: Multi-Provider LLM Support
epics.description: Abstraction layer supporting multiple LLM backends — local-first (Ollama, vLLM, llama.cpp) with optional cloud fallback (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral, Groq). Includes provider interface, routing, and fallback chain.
acceptance_criteria: Unified provider interface for all LLM backends
At least 3 local providers supported (Ollama, vLLM, llama.cpp)
At least 4 cloud providers supported (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Mistral/Groq)
Local-first fallback chain with automatic failover
RAG pipeline works with any provider via the abstraction layer

epics.title: AI Coding CLI & Agent Support
epics.description: Pre-installed, auto-configured AI coding tools — CLIs (Claude Code, Aider, Continue, Tabby, Open Interpreter), IDE plugins (Cursor, Copilot, Cody), and code agents (SWE-agent, Codex CLI, Mentat). All wired to the local LLM provider layer with RAG-enhanced project context.
acceptance_criteria: At least 5 coding CLIs installed and functional on ARM64
All tools auto-configured to use local LLM providers
OpenAI-compatible API endpoint for IDE plugin connectivity
Code agents run sandboxed with workspace mounting
Codebase indexing provides RAG context to all tools
Coding-optimized model presets auto-selected by device capability

epics.title: OS Foundation & Infrastructure
epics.description: Core OS infrastructure: bootable ARM64 image build pipeline, init system, boot ordering, filesystem layout, networking, security, CI/CD. The foundation everything else depends on.
acceptance_criteria: Buildroot produces bootable ARM64 image for at least one Tier 1 target
Full boot sequence: kernel → init → containerd → AI stack → controller
QEMU-based CI pipeline validates every commit
Container networking and service discovery functional
Security hardened: non-root containers, secrets management
Filesystem supports persistent model/vector storage and A/B updates

epics.title: Hardware Board Support Packages
epics.description: Board-specific support for Tier 1 hardware targets: Apple Silicon via Asahi Linux (CPU-first), Jetson Orin Nano Super (CUDA), RK3588 (RKNN NPU), Raspberry Pi 5 + Hailo-10H. Each BSP includes drivers, acceleration, and performance validation.
acceptance_criteria: BMT AI OS boots on all four Tier 1 targets
NPU/GPU acceleration functional per board
Performance benchmarks documented per board (tok/s for reference model)
Board-specific quirks documented

epics.title: Native Dashboard
epics.description: Native web dashboard for system monitoring, model management, RAG console, provider config, and log viewing. Built with Next.js + shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS. Dark theme default, responsive, real-time via WebSocket.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard accessible via http://device-ip:9090 from any browser
Built with Next.js App Router + shadcn/ui (new-york style) + Tailwind CSS
Real-time metrics via WebSocket (no polling)
Model management: browse, install, remove, switch
RAG console: ingest docs, query with source attribution
Provider configuration with drag-and-drop fallback chain (dnd-kit)
Live log viewer with virtualized scrolling (react-virtual)
Responsive design: mobile and desktop
Production build under 2MB gzipped, static export for ARM64
Dark theme default with next-themes toggle

epics.title: On-Device AI Training & Fine-Tuning
epics.description: Local model training and LoRA/QLoRA fine-tuning on edge hardware. PyTorch + Hugging Face stack with data preparation tools, Jupyter notebooks, TensorBoard monitoring, and model export to Ollama/GGUF. Train on Jetson (CUDA), run on all targets.
acceptance_criteria: PyTorch ARM64-native with CUDA support on Jetson
LoRA fine-tune a 1.5B model on Jetson Orin (8GB) in under 1 hour
QLoRA fine-tune same model using <2GB VRAM
Data preparation: docs → instruction-tuning dataset via CLI
Jupyter Lab for interactive training experiments
TensorBoard for training visualization
Export fine-tuned model directly to Ollama for serving
Full pipeline: prepare data → train → export → serve in one workflow

epics.title: Production Readiness & Security Hardening
epics.description: Address critical and high-priority findings from the production readiness evaluation. Fix auth defaults, error handling, input validation, CI gaps, secrets management, and test coverage.

epics.title: AI Assistant Memory & Conversations
epics.description: Transform BMT AI OS from a stateless LLM proxy into a conversational AI assistant with persistent sessions, context engine, memory search, and RAG-integrated chat. Inspired by OpenClaw's memory architecture.
acceptance_criteria: Chat completions persist conversation history in SQLite
Context engine assembles messages within token budget with auto-compaction
RAG results auto-injected into chat context when relevant
Hybrid BM25 + vector memory search with temporal decay
Conversation history API: list, search, export
